title: mentha × verticillata var. riparia
intro: Mentha × verticillata var. riparia, also known as American watermint or watermint, is a hybrid plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is native to North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The medicinal properties of Mentha × verticillata var. riparia are attributed to its high content of menthol and other volatile oils, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including headaches, nausea, and skin irritations. In herbal preparations, Mentha × verticillata var. riparia can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal benefits, making it a popular ingredient in natural remedies and holistic health practices.
